title: trond status output
description: Returned by `trond status <node> -o json`. Combines stored state with a best-effort live probe of the node's HTTP API.

last_applied.description: Timestamp of the last apply that modified state.

block_height.description: Latest block the node has processed; only present when status=running and the live HTTP probe succeeded.

peer_count.description: Number of currently connected peers; only present from live probe.

is_synced.description: True if block_height >= the network's perceived head. Derived from the same probe; absent if the probe failed.

lag.description: Block delta between local height and the highest height the node has seen across peers; only present from live probe.

probe_error.description: Set when the live probe couldn't reach the HTTP API (timed out / refused). status fields from state.json are still present.
